prompt: Surrealist dark fantasy fine art image, cinematic, hyperdetailed, Baroque style. Absolutely no text, numbers, labels, or written elements anywhere. A row of nine stone statues stands across the full width of the frame, each dramatically larger than the previous one — the size jump between each statue is extreme and obvious. THE LEFTMOST STATUE: Absolutely tiny — no bigger than a human hand — but it stands atop a magnificent impossibly grand cathedral throne of pure gold that dominates the entire left side of the image. Around this tiny statue, an absolutely overwhelming ocean of worshippers — tens of thousands of tiny human figures — crowd, kneel, and prostrate themselves in every direction, packed so densely they look like a single heaving living mass extending to the horizon. Blinding golden-white divine light erupts from the tiny statue like a supernova, illuminating everything on the left half of the image. This tiny figure is unmistakably the god of this world. THE PROGRESSION RIGHTWARD: Each subsequent statue is dramatically larger in body. But each has a smaller throne. Fewer worshippers — dropping off steeply, almost shockingly fast. The third statue has maybe a hundred followers. The fourth has a handful. The fifth has nobody. The light gets progressively colder and dimmer with each step rightward. THE RIGHTMOST STATUE: An absolute colossus — so enormous its head is cut off by the top of the frame, its shoulders wider than the middle statues are tall. It stands on bare empty ground — no throne, no pedestal, nothing. The space around its feet is vast, cold, completely empty. Not a single worshipper. Pure cold blue-grey darkness surrounds it. It is the largest being in existence and the most utterly forgotten and irrelevant. It stares into the void. Rembrandt chiaroscuro lighting, dark baroque surrealism, hyperdetailed. No text, no numbers, no symbols anywhere in the image.
visual_logic: Nine statues grow larger left to right, yet the tiniest commands a vast worshipping crowd and golden light, while the colossal rightmost stands completely alone in cold darkness — size inverts dominance.
concept: Benford's Law states that in naturally occurring data, smaller leading digits appear far more frequently than larger ones — the number 1 leads about 30% of the time while 9 leads less than 5%. The universe is biased toward beginnings and smallness, not magnitude.
metaphor: Nine statues of increasing size represent digits 1 through 9. The tiniest statue — digit 1 — sits on the grandest throne surrounded by an ocean of worshippers, embodying its overwhelming dominance in natural data. The colossal final statue — digit 9 — stands utterly alone in cold darkness, its great size meaning nothing, mirroring how rarely it appears as a leading digit.
